title: Fix navbar transparency and link visibility issues

requirements.text: Rewrite frontend/src/components/Navbar.tsx with a fully opaque navy (#0D1B4B) background that is never transparent, even when rendered over a dark hero image. The navbar must have a solid background-color at all times — do not use bg-transparent, bg-opacity-*, or any backdrop-blur that could cause the navbar content to visually disappear against dark page backgrounds. Set an explicit z-index (e.g. z-50) so the navbar always renders above page content.

quotes: still same problem home dashboard and admin for admin hide, i think this problem creates because of transparent navbar

acceptanceCriteria: Navbar has a solid opaque navy (#0D1B4B) background on all pages, including the Home page with its dark hero section
Navbar text and links are always clearly readable (white text on navy background) with no transparency artifacts
Navbar renders above all page content with no z-index conflicts

requirements.text: In frontend/src/components/Navbar.tsx, ensure all navigation links and the Login/Logout button are always visible and never clipped or hidden. The desktop nav must use a flex layout with flex-nowrap and sufficient horizontal spacing so all rendered items fit in a single row. The three conditional nav links must be: 'Home' (always visible, navigates to /), 'Dashboard' (visible only when authenticated, navigates to /dashboard), and 'Admin Panel' (visible only when authenticated AND isAdmin is true, navigates to /admin). The Login/Logout button must always appear on the far right. No link or button must overflow, wrap, or be hidden at any viewport width on desktop (md and above).

quotes: still same problem home dashboard and admin for admin hide

acceptanceCriteria: On desktop, 'Home', 'Dashboard' (when authenticated), 'Admin Panel' (when admin), and the Login/Logout button are all visible in a single horizontal row
No nav link or button is clipped, wrapped, or hidden at standard desktop viewport widths
The 'Admin Panel' link appears only for authenticated admin users and is hidden for non-admin users
'Dashboard' link appears only when the user is authenticated

requirements.text: In frontend/src/components/Navbar.tsx, fix the mobile hamburger menu to correctly display all conditional links against the opaque navy background. The mobile dropdown menu must have a solid opaque navy (#0D1B4B) background (not transparent) so links are readable. Include the same conditional link set as desktop: 'Home' (always), 'Dashboard' (authenticated only), 'Admin Panel' (authenticated + isAdmin only), plus the Login/Logout action. The hamburger icon and the mobile dropdown must be clearly visible on all page backgrounds including dark hero images.

quotes: still same problem home dashboard and admin for admin hide, i think this problem creates because of transparent navbar. please see what is happen to navbar and solve all problem

acceptanceCriteria: Mobile hamburger menu dropdown has a solid opaque navy background, not transparent
All conditional links are visible and readable in the mobile menu
Hamburger icon is visible on all page backgrounds
Mobile menu opens and closes reliably

requirements.text: In frontend/src/components/Navbar.tsx, ensure the useIsCallerAdmin query is called at the top level of the component. Derive isAdmin = data === true. Render the 'Admin Panel' link in both desktop nav and mobile hamburger menu only when the user is authenticated AND isAdmin === true AND the query is not in a loading/fetching state. Remove any console.log or debug statements from prior iterations. The three nav links must be exactly: 'Home' (always visible), 'Dashboard' (authenticated only), 'Admin Panel' (authenticated + isAdmin only).
acceptanceCriteria: Admin Panel link appears for the admin user in both desktop and mobile nav after login
Admin Panel link does not appear for non-admin authenticated users
Admin Panel link does not flicker or appear briefly for non-admin users during loading
No console.log debug statements remain in the component

constraints: Do not modify any files listed in frontend.immutablePaths
Navbar background must always be solid opaque navy (#0D1B4B) — never transparent or semi-transparent
Timer, question navigation, submission logic, and Back to Dashboard button on Test page must remain unchanged
nonGoals: Changing any page content outside the Navbar component
Changing the test-taking flow or question display
Adding new navigation links beyond the three specified (Home, Dashboard, Admin Panel)
